reviews.review_description:
We have been using Arkio on the Meta Quest 2, Meta Quest 3 and Meta Quest 3S for the past year and a half.  My team and I started by reviewing architectural models in VR, together in the same space, and have now been using the Mixed Reality portions on site together with clients.

It is quite incredible that we're able to review a virtual design while being physically in different locations.  Each week, we host meetings to review the work to date, and some staff have their headsets on working from home, while others might be in the studio.

For the Mixed Reality - this has become more essential in the early design phases as clients typically are unable to visualize spaces based on hard-lined drawings (and even renderings).  By having them "step into" the design through Mixed Reality or Virtual Reality, their expectations are set on the feeling of a volume or space.

More critically, as an architect, this program has become an essential tool that helps provide design feedback more quickly.   Typically, an architect would design a project, and then would have to get to the framing or rough construction phase to actually feel a volume of space in person.   Many projects do not ever see the light of day, let alone get to that point.

By utilizing Arkio, my staff are able to increase their feedback loop in practice, thus making them masters of space in a shorter amount of time and training.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! It's great to hear how Arkio is enabling you and your team to design, visualize and collaborate with clients more effectively!

reviews.review_description:
Arkio is an impressive platform for immersive design collaboration. The ability to explore projects in real scale using VR significantly improves spatial understanding and communication of complex ideas.

As an engineering professional and postgraduate student, I find Arkio particularly valuable for conceptual design review, spatial analysis, and presenting infrastructure and construction solutions in a much more intuitive way.

The interface is intuitive, performance is smooth even with large models, and the collaborative features open many possibilities for design discussions and project development.

Highly recommended for architects, engineers, and anyone interested in spatial computing and immersive design workflows.

reviews.review_title: Best Tool for our University Students
reviews.review_description: Our students from the Built Environment loves this app the most when it comes to viewing, ideation, and walkthroughs for their models. We had a great experience and see so much benefit for this app.

developer_response.body: Great to hear Arkio is working well for you and your students!

reviews.review_description: Meta Quest 3 - App cannot run well enough from launch to even interact with the menu.

developer_response.body: Hi, it sounds like something is going wrong with Arkio on your device. If you can get in touch with us at support@arkio.is, we can work together to identify what is going wrong and fix this issue. Thanks!

reviews.review_description: I've been trying for months to do something useful with this thing. In regards to trying to make a useful model of my apartment, it has just been a huge waste of time. Making models and trying to import them, trying to use the meta room setup tool and import the scene to arkio also sucked hugely.  Trying to just make the model in arkio. It just sucks all around, its buggy as hell. Good Luck.

developer_response.body:
We’re sorry to hear how frustrating your experience has been. That’s definitely not the experience we want anyone to have with Arkio.

Creating an accurate apartment model can involve several steps, whether you import from other design tools, use room setup features, or build directly in Arkio. We understand how frustrating it can be when things feel buggy. We’re continuously improving stability and workflows, and feedback like yours genuinely helps us identify where things aren’t working as they should.

If you’re open to it, we’d really appreciate the chance to learn more about what didn’t work for you so we can help troubleshoot or improve the experience. Please feel free to reach out to us at support@arkio.is

reviews.review_description: This is one of the best solutions for startup whith virtual reality. You have all possibilities to build virtual city for instance and make projects. I am using software in combination whith Blender and it works extremely good even with lot of objects inside. It's simple and it would be even better whith simple animation possibilities. I worm recommend it for school projects.

Hey lads, good sw! I have one point that can make it even better, do you think that it is possible to scan the room with the photo textures on the walls ? I am usualy drawing existing buildings electrity wirings im autocad and this would be awesome if i could just “teleport” my self to the building that i am drawing to see the socket and light placement 😁

I learned how to use this with little instruction in an afternoon. Built a little house in that time frame not fully complete but still. Has a few issues with some controls (intuitive button use) and menu gets in the way sometimes, but you get used to it. Both hands can have seperate functions like creating and editing making stuff faster.

reviews.review_title: VERY difficult to use in VR
reviews.review_description: To build/design a room from a VR scan or from scratch using a Meta Quest 3 is nothing shy of infuriating. Walls, windows, openings, ceilings will not be placed where you need them to be, there doesn't appear to be any kitchen cabinets to firnish a kitchen, navigating around the design with VR is incredibly difficult. Ideally it needs to be designed on a PC and then viewed with a Quest VR device. Otherwise, like me, you may lose hours and hours to this! Concept is good, app is not.

I haven't played much, but oh man it's fun to play around with! It's free so I don't have any room to complain. Not sure if it's something that I will use much, but maybe once I get better at it. I've been 3D modeling for about 20 years and it's nice to see what VR, AR, and mixed reality are innovating in this field.
It is a great game I designed my real house and it dose not cost money that’s the great part it’s such a good game I’m so happy they have this

reviews.review_title: This is clearly just for walkthrough...
reviews.review_description:
It's veeery difficult to build a precise building. The snapping is really bad, grouping objects together also doesn't work properly (couldn't figure out how to ungroup objects as well) - in such case I'm unable to create multi level building. The building process in VR is just awful, same with painting/ texturing/ placing objects. I guess there are some issues with raycasting, sometimes it just hits random objects all over the map.

However I believe this might be a cool app for presenting already built buildings and this app already has it. All you need to do is to upload your asset (image, fbx or obj) and download it directly in the app. But I don't recommend editing anything through the app. Also UI is horribly bad! 1.5/5 just for the "walkthrough" feature!

developer_response.body:
We appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts. We’re sorry to hear that Arkio felt difficult to use.

Creating complex designs, such as multistory buildings, can be challenging for our mobile solid modeling engine. We are constantly improving this with updates we made over the last few months, which we hope have addressed some of your issues and confusion about the user interface.

If you’re open to it, we’d love to better understand what felt confusing or challenging so we can improve it. Feel free to reach out at support@arkio.is

reviews.review_description:
Great collaboration with Arkio, app allows our students in Rezekne Academy of Technologies to work in design study programs, great new possibilities for working in VR in collaboration and also importing student designs. 
The support and communication was great and we recommend the app to education institutions who are starting to work with VR.

This VR app for collaborative 3D design is a transformative tool for students. Its intuitive interface, allowing easy 3D model import and creation, sets the stage for a truly remarkable collaborative experience. Working alongside classmates in the same virtual space, manipulating and crafting 3D objects in real-time, brings teamwork to new heights. The comprehensive set of design tools, immersive VR experience, and compatibility with various headsets cater to students of all skill levels. This app redefines how students collaborate and design in 3D, making it an invaluable addition to any educational toolkit, particularly for those in design, architecture, or related fields. It's a five-star game-changer.
Game is really fun and addictive I can play this for ever sooo I GOTTA GIVE YA GUYS A 5

reviews.review_title: This software opens up new opportunities
reviews.review_description:
This is a really high-quality tool for facilitating design meetings for game development. Scenes can be mocked up quickly with the well designed tools and you can export these scenes to useful formats, including directly to Unity.

As it's cross-platform with tablet and PC users, and with their experiences and edit capabilities just as intuitive and useful, it's proving to be an interesting new communication tool for me, and I'm looking forward to using it more in my workflows.

developer_response.body: Glad to hear, we look forward to seeing how you continue to incorporate Arkio into your workflow!

reviews.review_description: I like this because i can make structures for minecraft and ot really gives me ideas s recommended for anyone that like to do architecture👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻😀😀😀

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback, great to hear you're enjoying Arkio and discovering new workflows!

reviews.review_description: I open the game and I see Arkio then app close

developer_response.body: (UPDATE: Issue resolved by user) Sorry to hear you're having problems running Arkio. We would appreciate if you would get in touch with us at support@arkio.is so we can help figure out what the problem is. Hope to hear from you soon!

reviews.review_description: I couldn't figure out if I was supposed to use the green platform on my floor to use to edit ON or to use it to edit in my passthrough. I skipped the tutorial bcuz It didn't help at all-so In all formed in conclusion I didn't get it I got so frustrated I woke up my house (3:00) but if I wasn't so frustrated I could have got it I also didn't get the overestimulating amount of mechanics this is not to blame the game its to blame me (½)

Arkio was used during a master thesis. The subject focused on the contribution of BIM in a participatory approach. A virtual model was made using Revit then exported with Arkio. This virtual model was then used in participatory workshops to design urban developments with the inhabitants.
Following this experimentation, Arkio turned out to be a super, very intuitive tool. The inhabitants, although not used to Oculus, quickly took control of the software. It has been possible to create high quality projects thanks to Arkio.
It is an excellent collaborative tool that allows you to clear up many misunderstandings and produce a 3D project in record time.
It is obvious that the project produced remains at the level of a feasibility model but the export solution makes it possible to transform this quickly under Revit for a specific project.
